top_signals.title: Growing CRE Opportunities Outside of Data Centers: Chen
top_signals.summary: Morris Chen, portfolio manager CBMS/CRE debt at DoubleLine, joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss the evolution in the financing market, the bifurcated recovery of the commercial real estate market, and the risks investing in data centers. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Potential liquidity or banking-system instability affecting financial conditions.

top_signals.title: The Growing Population of Zombie ETF
top_signals.summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's Athanasios Psarofagis joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss the growing population of zombie ETFs, which are defined as funds that are at least three years old and manage less than $50 million.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: LTL general rate increases no longer annual
top_signals.summary:
ArcBest announced a 5.9% general rate increase for less-than-truckload services, which is about six weeks ahead of its last rate bump.

The post LTL general rate increases no longer annual appeared first on FreightWaves.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, April 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income decreased less than $0.1 billion (less than 0.1 percent at a monthly rate) in April, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-decreased $19.9 billion (0.1 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $111.1 billion (0.5 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $114.0 billion in April. Personal saving was $611.7 billion in April, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 2.6 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Ericsson, Epiroc expand alliance to accelerate mining automation
top_signals.summary: Under the agreement, Epiroc will offer Ericsson’s LTE and 5G connectivity solutions as part of its digital portfolio for both surface and underground mines.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: EU unveils tech sovereignty package to cut reliance on US, Chinese suppliers
top_signals.summary: The package bundles two draft laws — a Chips Act 2.0 and a Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA) — alongside an Open Source Strategy and a roadmap for digitalizing the energy system.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: GDP (Second Estimate) and Corporate Profits, 1st Quarter 2026
top_signals.summary: Real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at an annual rate of 1.6 percent in the first quarter of 2026 (January, February, and March), according to the second estimate released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. In the fourth quarter of 2025, real GDP increased 0.5 percent. The contributors to the increase in real GDP in the first quarter were exports, investment, consumer spending, and government spending. Imports, which are a subtraction in the calculation of GDP, increased. Full Text

top_signals.title: UK gives big tech 3 months to create device controls to block nude images of kids
top_signals.summary: The companies “must activate built-in features or implement technical solutions on smartphones and tablets to detect and block nude images for children,” according to a press release from the Home Office. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced the measure in a speech at London Tech Week Monday.
